evidence: Paper reports variable importance from Random Forest: "Important trace elements for the classification based on variable importance were W, Ba, Mo, K and Cr." However, this ranking appears once in Results as a descriptive detail. Main conclusions center on predictive accuracy (94.5% within 100 km for combined method), spatial precision, and method complementarity—all supported by held-out test validation (25 random 41-tree test sets), not by feature importance rankings. Discussion explains multi-element success via mechanistic drivers (soil properties variation) and comparative performance metrics, never invoking the importance ranking. Conclusions would hold identically if importance were measured as permutation or SHAP.
